title: activity — data retention windows
description: Retention per model. Activity rows: 90d default (all tiers); extendable to 365d with `activity_retention_extended` (Enterprise); up to 730d for regulator holds. Retention policies: retained while active. Cold-storage archive at 90d for Enterprise tenants; hard-delete at retention_days for every tenant. TenantErased: cascade delete all activity rows (unlike audit which anonymises).

retention.description: Default 90-day retention. Rows past 90d are hard-deleted by PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob nightly. No soft-delete window — activity is a user-facing feed; there's no admin recovery path.

Extended retention via ActivityRetentionPolicy. Rows past 90d are moved to cold storage by ArchiveActivitiesToColdStorageJob weekly. Rows past retention_days are hard-deleted from cold storage by the retention job. Requires `activity_retention_extended` entitlement (Enterprise).
Regulator-hold retention. SHOULD carry an expires_at on the policy so the override doesn't drift to permanent. Rows past 90d in cold storage; rows past retention_days hard-deleted.
retention.erasureOnRequest: P0D (subject-request erasure still fires; regulator hold does NOT block GDPR Art. 17 — the tenant must document the conflict in a legal hold record)

retention.description: Active policies retained indefinitely — they govern retention behaviour on live data.
Soft-deleted policies retained 1 year for audit visibility ('why was retention set to X during time period Y'). Then hard-deleted.
audit rows on the retention policy (via HasAudit trait) retain 7 years per SOC 2 evidence retention. Owned by audit module.

complianceTiers.user_activity_data: User-visible feed data. Retained for the tenant's operational benefit. Purged automatically past the retention window. Erasable on subject request.
complianceTiers.user_activity_data_regulator_hold: Same as user_activity_data but with a regulator-hold flag. Retention extended per policy; erasure still fires but the tenant must record the conflict with the hold in a legal record.
complianceTiers.tenant_configuration: Tenant-owned configuration state. Retained while active; short soft-delete window for audit trace.
complianceTiers.audit_log: Immutable audit records on tenant configuration mutations.

identityErasure.description: When a User's Identity is erased under GDPR Art. 17, this module cascades: every activity WHERE causer OR subject is a User linked to the erased identity is HARD-DELETED. Activity does NOT anonymise (unlike audit) — activity rows are user-facing narrative and preserving them post-erasure would leak the erased identity's actions on other users' feeds.
identityErasure.note: Compare with audit: audit anonymises subject_id → 'erased-<hash>' but preserves rows for compliance evidence. Activity purges outright because the surface is user-facing narrative, not regulator record.

tenantErasure.description: When a Tenant is erased, EVERY activity row in the tenant is hard-deleted via the tenant_id CASCADE FK. Cold-storage archives for the tenant are also purged.

subjectErasure.description: When a User is soft-deleted (not identity-erased — just account soft-delete), activity rows attributed to them as CAUSER get their causer_id nulled. The row stays; the causer reference becomes NULL. This is a narrative-preservation choice — a booking's activity ('Alice booked Court 3 for tomorrow') stays even after Alice's account is soft-deleted; the row just renders 'Someone booked Court 3'.

legalHold.description: When compliance places a legal hold on a tenant / entity, related activity rows get a `hold_until` marker (via metadata) so the purge pipeline skips them. Hold placement + release fires SuspendPurgeForLegalHold + ResumePurgeAfterLegalHold listeners.
